content: For years, she was my constant companion—an imaginary friend in dreams, vivid and real, like a secret only I knew. We laughed, talked, shared moments no one else could see. I thought it was just my mind playing tricks, a childhood fancy fading with time. But when I saw her standing across the street years later, grown and real, my breath caught. Her eyes locked on mine, wide with recognition, then she turned swiftly and disappeared into the crowd. I chased her, heart pounding, desperate for answers. Who was she? How could she remember me? Why avoid me now? I reached out, but she vanished, leaving only questions. Was it all in my head, or had something real slipped through the cracks of my dreams? I couldn’t shake the feeling that whatever she was hiding wasn’t just from me—but from herself too.
